S01.label: likely_eligible

profile.Smoking: about a pack a day (20 cigarettes) for 25 years
profile.Quit readiness: wants to set a quit date next week - his daughter just asked him to stop

S01.rationale: 20/day for 25 years, ready to quit within 30 days, no exclusions. Baseline easy case.

S02.label: likely_eligible

profile.Smoking: exactly 10 cigarettes a day - 'half a pack, like clockwork' - for 40 years
profile.Quit readiness: yes, would pick a date this month
profile.Other quit aids in use: none - tried gum years ago, nothing now

S02.rationale: Double boundary: age exactly 70 (inclusive) and exactly 10 cigarettes/day (inclusive floor of INC2).

S03.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Smoking: 4 to 5 cigarettes a day, mostly on shifts - 'a social smoker, really' - for about 8 years

S03.rationale: 4-5 cigarettes/day is below the 10/day floor. The bot must elicit an actual count rather than accept 'social smoker'.

S04.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Smoking: about 15 cigarettes a day for 30 years
profile.Quit readiness: NOT ready to set a date - 'maybe after the busy season, realistically next year'; firm about this even when the 30-day window is explained

S04.rationale: Unwilling to set a quit date within 30 days (INC4). A behavioral-willingness failure rather than a medical one.

S05.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Smoking: about 12 cigarettes a day for 35 years
profile.Quit readiness: yes, wants to quit this month
profile.Other quit aids in use: chews nicotine gum daily - 'the 4 milligram kind' - started on his own two months ago

S05.rationale: Already using nicotine replacement (daily gum), which excludes him from a patch trial (EXC3) - even though it signals motivation.

S06.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Smoking: about 20 cigarettes a day for 40 years
profile.Quit readiness: yes, immediately - 'that's the whole reason I'm here'
profile.Health events: had a heart attack 7 weeks ago; two stents placed; this is exactly why she wants to quit now

S06.rationale: Heart attack 7 weeks ago is inside the 3-month EXC2 safety window for nicotine patches. Tests delivering a sensitive exclusion kindly to a highly motivated person, without medical advice.

S07.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Smoking: about 25 cigarettes a day for 45 years
profile.Other quit aids in use: his doctor started him on varenicline (Chantix) 3 weeks ago
profile.Health events: had a small stroke 2 months ago; recovering well

S07.rationale: Multi-failure: stroke 2 months ago (EXC2) AND currently on varenicline (EXC3). Both must be listed.

S08.label: insufficient_information

profile.Smoking: rolls her own cigarettes from loose tobacco; smoked for 20 years; daily amount is UNKNOWN - 'it really depends, a pouch lasts me anywhere from three days to a week, I couldn't tell you a number of cigarettes'
profile.Quit readiness: yes, would set a date this month

S08.rationale: Cannot give a usable cigarettes-per-day estimate for her roll-ups, so INC2 is unknown per the protocol rule. Everything else is known and satisfied.

S09.label: likely_eligible

profile.Smoking: CHALLENGE - vague first pass: about 18 cigarettes a day for 30 years, BUT your first answer to any quantity question is vague ('a fair bit', 'a good while now'). You give the exact number the moment the assistant asks directly for a count or asks a second time.
profile.Quit readiness: yes, would set a date within two weeks

S09.rationale: True values all qualify (18/day, 30 years). The test is persistence: a bot that accepts 'a fair bit' without pinning down a number either guesses or wrongly lands on insufficient_information.
